When a developer's recent PR review activity reaches the 95th percentile or higher among CodeRabbit users, additional reviews become available more gradually as earlier reviews age out of the rolling window. > > Please refer [docs](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#rate-limits) for additional details. > >

--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces configurable actions for two-finger tap gestures and stylus side buttons, updating preferences, canvas views, and settings panels to support these options. The review feedback suggests checking the return value of matrix.invert() to safely handle non-invertible matrices and adding vertical padding to the settings radio group options to improve touch target accessibility.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `matrix.invert(inv)` call returns a boolean indicating whether the matrix is invertible. If the matrix is singular (not invertible), `inv` will not be updated, which can lead to incorrect coordinates when calling `inv.mapPoints(pts)`. It is safer to check the return value of `invert` before proceeding.

```kotlin
                                        val inv = Matrix()
                                        if (matrix.invert(inv)) {
                                            val pts =
                                                floatArrayOf(
                                                    (twoFingerStartPt1[0] + twoFingerStartPt2[0]) / 2f,
                                                    (twoFingerStartPt1[1] + twoFingerStartPt2[1]) / 2f,
                                                )
                                            inv.mapPoints(pts)
                                            viewScope.launch { canvasController.paste(pts[0], pts[1]) }
                                        }
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The options in the radio group do not have any padding, which makes their touch targets very small and hard to tap, especially on E-Ink devices. Adding vertical padding inside the clickable area will significantly improve accessibility and usability.

```kotlin
            Row(
                modifier =
                    Modifier
                        .fillMaxWidth()
                        .clickable { onSelect(value) }
                        .padding(vertical = 8.dp),
                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
            )
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

```kotlin
                                        if (matrix.invert(inv)) {
```

The options in the radio group do not have any padding, which makes their touch targets very small and hard to tap, especially on E-Ink devices. Adding vertical padding inside the clickable area will significantly improve accessibility and usability.

